content.thinking:
Now I have a complete understanding of the problem:

## Root Cause Analysis

The issue is that the **Editor component renders ALL lines without any maximum height constraint**. When `setEditorText` is called with text that produces more lines than the terminal can display:

1. The `Editor.render()` method generates ALL `layoutLines` (see line 325-390 in editor.ts)
2. The TUI's `doRender()` outputs ALL these lines sequentially with `\r\n`
3. When total lines exceed terminal height, the terminal scrolls unpredictably
4. The cursor tracking (`cursorRow`) becomes incorrect
5. Subsequent renders corrupt the display because differential rendering assumes lines stay in place

## Evidence from the Screenshot

The image shows the classic "double-rendering" artifact where:
- Lines appear to be written twice (shifted/overlapping)
- Content wraps back around after going off-screen
- The state tracking is completely broken

## Why ESC Stops Working

ESC stops working because:
1. The input handling depends on `handleInput()` being called on the focused component
2. When the TUI's cursor tracking is broken, the rendering gets into a bad state
3. The TUI may not be correctly forwarding input, OR the component itself is in a bad state

However, Ctrl+C still works because it's handled at a lower level (SIGINT).

## Proposed Fix

The Editor component needs a **maximum height constraint with vertical scrolling**. Here's the approach:

### Option A: Add maxHeight to Editor (Recommended)

1. Add `maxHeight?: number` parameter to Editor (passed during render or set externally)
2. Track a `scrollOffset` for vertical scrolling
3. In `render()`, only output lines from `scrollOffset` to `scrollOffset + maxHeight`
4. Ensure the cursor line is always visible by adjusting `scrollOffset`
5. The cursor movement methods need to call a method to ensure the cursor is in view


6. Alternatively, the TUI could pass available height to components, but this requires changing the Component interface and is less ideal.

### Implementation Details (Option A)

I need to add state variables for `maxLines` (defaulting to Infinity, configurable) and `scrollOffset` (starting at 0) to track the viewport. Then modify `render()` to slice the `layoutLines` array between `scrollOffset` and `scrollOffset + maxLines`, calling `ensureCursorVisible()` first to adjust the scroll position so the cursor stays in view.

The `ensureCursorVisible()` method should locate which layout line has the cursor, then adjust `scrollOffset` upward if the cursor is above the current view or downward if it's below. I'll also add a `setMaxLines()` setter for external control, which the interactive mode can call after calculating the available terminal height minus space for other UI elements.

### Affected Files

Changes needed in `packages/tui/src/components/editor.ts` for the scrolling logic, and potentially `packages/coding-agent/src/modes/interactive/interactive-mode.ts` to set the editor's max height based on terminal dimensions.

The cleanest approach avoids breaking the Component interface—instead of modifying `render(width: number)` to accept height, I'll add an optional `maxLines` property directly to the Editor class. This follows the existing pattern used by SelectList with its `maxVisible` parameter, keeping the implementation consistent with other height-constrained components in the codebase.

content.text:
# Changelog

## [Unreleased]

### Added

- New `input` event in extension system for intercepting, transforming, or handling user input before the agent processes it. Supports three result types: `continue` (pass through), `transform` (modify text/images), `handled` (respond without LLM). Handlers chain transforms and short-circuit on handled. ([#761](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/pull/761) by [@nicobailon](https://github.com/nicobailon))
- Extension example: `input-transform.ts` demonstrating input interception patterns (quick mode, instant commands, source routing) ([#761](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/pull/761) by [@nicobailon](https://github.com/nicobailon))

### Changed

- Skill commands (`/skill:name`) are now expanded in AgentSession instead of interactive mode. This enables skill commands in RPC and print modes, and allows the `input` event to intercept `/skill:name` before expansion.

### Fixed

- Piped stdin now works correctly: `echo foo | pi` is equivalent to `pi -p foo`. When stdin is piped, print mode is automatically enabled since interactive mode requires a TTY ([#708](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/issues/708))
- Session tree now preserves branch connectors and indentation when filters hide intermediate entries so descendants attach to the nearest visible ancestor and sibling branches align. Fixed in both TUI and HTML export ([#739](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/pull/739) by [@w-winter](https://github.com/w-winter))
- Added `upstream connect`, `connection refused`, and `reset before headers` patterns to auto-retry error detection ([#733](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/issues/733))

## [0.46.0] - 2026-01-15

### Fixed

- Scoped models (`--models` or `enabledModels`) now remember the last selected model across sessions instead of always starting with the first model in the scope ([#736](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/pull/736) by [@ogulcancelik](https://github.com/ogulcancelik))
- Show `bun install` instead of `npm install` in update notification when running under Bun ([#714](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/pull/714) by [@dannote](https://github.com/dannote))
- `/skill` prompts now include the skill path ([#711](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/pull/711) by [@jblwilliams](https://github.com/jblwilliams))
- Use configurable `expandTools` keybinding instead of hardcoded Ctrl+O ([#717](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/pull/717) by [@dannote](https://github.com/dannote))
- Compaction turn prefix summaries now merge correctly ([#738](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/pull/738) by [@vsabavat](https://github.com/vsabavat))
- Avoid unsigned Gemini 3 tool calls ([#741](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/pull/741) by [@roshanasingh4](https://github.com/roshanasingh4))
- Fixed signature support for non-Anthropic models in Amazon Bedrock provider ([#727](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/pull/727) by [@unexge](https://github.com/unexge))

## Summary

**Branch:** `fix/editor-overflow-scrolling`

**Root Cause:** The `Editor` component rendered all layout lines without any height constraint. When `setEditorText` loaded text larger than the terminal, lines overflowed causing terminal corruption and input handling failures.

**Fix:** Added vertical scrolling to the Editor component:

1. **`packages/tui/src/components/editor.ts`**
   - Constructor now requires `TUI` as first parameter for terminal dimension access
   - Added `scrollOffset` tracking
   - `render()` now calculates max visible lines from `tui.terminal.rows`
   - Cursor is kept visible by adjusting scroll offset
   - Top/bottom borders show scroll indicators: `─── ↑ 5 more ───`

2. **`packages/coding-agent/src/modes/interactive/components/custom-editor.ts`**
   - Updated constructor signature to pass TUI

3. **Updated all Editor instantiation sites:**
   - `interactive-mode.ts`
   - `extension-editor.ts`
   - `question.ts`, `questionnaire.ts`, `modal-editor.ts`, `rainbow-editor.ts` examples
   - `editor.test.ts` (41 tests, all passing)
   - `chat-simple.ts`

4. **Documentation:**
   - Updated `packages/tui/README.md` with new API
   - Added changelog entries for breaking change and fix

summary:
## Brief
Issue #732 is fully resolved and merged to main. The Editor component now has vertical scrolling when content exceeds terminal height. The session ended with removing the temporary test extension `load-file.ts` that was created for testing the scrolling feature.

## User intent trail
1. **Analyze issue #732**: "Prompts larger than the screen fuck up everything" - editor corrupts when `setEditorText` loads large text
2. User clarified that Editor should get TUI via constructor: "ok, then lets have the editor (and customeditor) receive Tui as a parameter to the constructor (first parameter)"
3. "no backward compatibility" - TUI parameter must be required, not optional
4. "create a feature branch, then go to town" - implement the fix
5. After initial implementation: "this is alsmost good, but the max editor height is too high, should be 30% of terminal height at most, minimally 5 lines i think"
6. Asked for Page Up/Down support; confirmed MacBook uses Fn+Up/Down for these keys
7. When Page Up/Down didn't work: "well, cool, but they don't trigger" - led to discovering the case sensitivity bug
8. After fix: "page up/down now work"
9. "yes, merge to main, close issue with comment in my tone"
10. "i assume you removed the load-file.ts extension?" → "yes, remove"

## Constraints & preferences
- TUI is required first parameter to Editor (no backward compatibility/optional parameter)
- Editor max height: 30% of terminal rows, minimum 5 lines
- User tone for issue comments: direct, technical, no fluff

## Errors, fixes, and dead ends
- **Page Up/Down keys not triggering**: `parseKeyId()` lowercases key identifiers, so `"pageUp"` → `"pageup"`, but switch cases were camelCase `case "pageUp":`. Fixed by changing to `case "pageup":` and `case "pagedown":`
- **Biome lint warning about useless constructor in rainbow-editor.ts**: Removed the now-unnecessary constructor that only called super with same args
- **Unused imports after removing constructor**: Cleaned up unused `EditorTheme`, `TUI`, `KeybindingsManager` imports from rainbow-editor.ts

## Key decisions
- **TUI as first parameter**: Enables Editor to query `tui.terminal.rows` for dynamic height calculation
- **30% max height, min 5 lines**: Balances visibility of content with keeping other UI elements visible
- **Protected `tui` field in Editor**: Changed from `private` to `protected` so subclasses like RainbowEditor can access it without declaring their own
- **Scroll indicators**: Top/bottom borders show `─── ↑ N more ───` when content is scrolled

## Status
- [DONE] Editor vertical scrolling with max height constraint
- [DONE] Page Up/Down key support (`pageUp`/`pageDown` actions in keybindings)
- [DONE] Fixed case sensitivity bug in keys.ts for pageup/pagedown
- [DONE] Updated all Editor instantiation sites (41 test cases, interactive-mode, extension-editor, extension examples)
- [DONE] Updated changelogs (tui, coding-agent) and README
- [DONE] Merged branch `fix/editor-overflow-scrolling` to main, pushed
- [DONE] Issue #732 closed with comment
- [DONE] Removed test extension `load-file.ts`
- [DONE] Deleted feature branch

## Task continuity
No active tasks. All work for issue #732 is complete and merged.

## Open issues & uncertainties
- VS Code terminal and macOS Terminal don't send Page Up/Down sequences (noted but acceptable - these terminals are not officially supported)

## Immediate next steps
None - work is complete. User's final request "yes, remove" was fulfilled by deleting `load-file.ts` and pushing to main.

## Mandatory reading
If resuming work on editor/keys:
- `packages/tui/src/components/editor.ts` - scrolling logic in `render()`, `pageScroll()` method
- `packages/tui/src/keys.ts` - key matching with lowercase case labels
- `packages/tui/src/keybindings.ts` - `pageUp`/`pageDown` action definitions
